title: melittis melissophyllum var. albida
intro: Melittis melissophyllum var. albida, also known as the white honeywort, is a perennial plant species in the borage family that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The white honeywort has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and bronchodilator properties, as well as its ability to soothe digestive issues and reduce fever. The plant can be used in herbal preparations such as infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
